speaker: Narrator
text: It's early morning. Rain taps against your window, steady and soft. You get up to make coffee and notice something outside: a tiny orange kitten, huddled under the eaves, fur matted from the rain.

choices.text: Go outside to check on it

speaker: Narrator
text: You step outside with an umbrella. The kitten flinches at first, then looks up at you with round, golden eyes. It's shivering. It can't be more than a few weeks old.

speaker: You
text: You watch through the glass. The kitten tries to shake off the rain, sneezes twice, then lets out a meow so small you almost miss it. Something tugs at your chest.

speaker: Narrator
text: You carry the kitten inside and wrap it in a warm towel. For a moment it goes still. Then you feel it: a tiny rumble against your hands. It's purring.

speaker: Narrator
text: You set a small bowl of warm milk by the door. The kitten sniffs it once, twice, then drinks eagerly. When the bowl is empty, it sits and looks up at you, as if to say: what now?

speaker: Neighbor
text: You ask around the neighborhood all afternoon. Nobody recognizes it. An old neighbor on the corner smiles. 'No collar, no chip. Looks like that little one chose you.'

speaker: Narrator
text: Evening. The rain has stopped. You sit on the floor while the kitten sleeps curled in your lap, paws twitching in a dream. A ribbon of golden light slips through the window. Some mornings change everything.
